data.body:
Calibration: f8b7-4e3f (Move version bumping to merge time) — expected PASS: All criteria met.

SC1 (two worktrees merge sequentially with no conflict markers): PASS — Behavioral guarantee provided by _phase_version_bump reading version from main after pull. Architecture correct; no conflict path remains.

SC2 (every merge where version.file_path configured produces one increment; absent = skip silently): PASS — _phase_version_bump in merge-to-main.sh (line 1039+) checks BUMP_TYPE; when absent, logs 'INFO: --bump not specified -- skipping version bump' and returns. version.file_path drives bump-version.sh invocation.

SC3 (merge-to-main.sh --bump flag with patch/minor; reads from main after pull): PASS — --bump flag present (lines 1263-1266); _phase_version_bump runs after _phase_merge (after pull). bump-version.sh invoked with BUMP_TYPE.

SC4 (tag-release.sh standalone; does not invoke merge-to-main.sh): PASS — tag-release.sh is a separate script; no cross-invocation.

SC5 (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md Step 3b removed): PASS — grep of COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md finds no 'bump' or 'patch version' steps.

SC6 (Sprint Step 13 replaced with --bump minor to merge-to-main.sh): PASS — sprint SKILL.md references merge-to-main.sh --bump minor.

Overall: all 6 criteria PASS. Completion verifier would correctly return overall_verdict: PASS.
